title: Watch Out for Ticks! Doctors Say This Could Be a Bad Year for Lyme Disease
summary: Have you ever found a tiny bug on your skin after playing outside? That might be a tick — and doctors say this year, there could be a lot of them! Ticks are small, eight-legged creatures that drink blood. They can carry diseases like Lyme disease, which makes people very sick. The CDC (Centers for Disease Control) says more people are going to the ER for tick bites than any year since 2017. That's a big deal! Why are there so many ticks? One reason is climate change — ticks like warm, wet weather. Also, there have been a lot of mice in the last two years, and ticks love to feed on mice. In Connecticut, people are already sending in 30 ticks a day for testing! The worst part is that tiny baby ticks, called nymphs, are hard to see. They can stay on you for days and make you sick. So what can you do? Wear light-colored clothes, use bug spray, and stay in the middle of paths when you walk in the woods. After playing outside, check your skin for ticks. If you find one, pull it off right away. Stay safe and have fun outside!
why_it_matters: When you play outside in grassy or wooded areas, you need to know how to check for ticks to stay healthy.

questions.question: Why are there more ticks this year?
options: Because of climate change and many mice
questions.correct_answer: Because of climate change and many mice

questions.question: What should you do after playing outside?
Where in the US are people sending in many ticks for testing?

background_read: Ticks are found in grassy and wooded areas around the world.
Lyme disease was first discovered in the 1970s in Connecticut (a state in the northeastern US).
Climate change is making some places warmer, which helps tick populations increase.
Article_Structure: WHO: Doctors and the CDC (Centers for Disease Control).
WHAT: They warn that this year could have a lot of ticks and Lyme disease cases.
WHERE: Especially in Connecticut, but also across the United States where ticks live.
WHY: Because warm weather from climate change and a lot of mice have made more ticks.

perspectives.description: They want to warn people so they can prevent tick bites and Lyme disease.
They might be worried but can learn simple steps to stay safe, like using bug spray.
They need to remind children to check for ticks after playing outside and know how to remove them.
